_comment: This file is OVERWRITTEN each security review. Do NOT append to it.

ssh_attacks.fail2ban_status: active - sshd jail running, 1 currently banned, 2778 total banned
ssh_attacks.change_since_last: Failed attempts at 10065 in current log. fail2ban total bans 2778 (up from 2710, +68). 1 IP currently banned (was 9). Standard botnet scanning continues with no targeted attacks. Only authorized logins from known IP (109.81.31.32) via publickey.

pdf_security.innerHTML_note: 22 usages across viewer.js, upload.js, merge.js, annotate.js, pages.js, split.js - all static templates, clearing operations, static SVGs, or integer-only interpolation. No user data injected unsafely.

split_module_review.innerHTML_note: 6 usages: clearing grid, static SVG checkboxes, range row template with integer-only interpolation (split.rangeCount), button restores with static SVG. No user data in any innerHTML.
split_module_review.user_data_handling: Range input parsed via parseRange() with strict validation (only digits, commas, dashes). Filenames built from sanitizeFilename(). Page numbers are integers only.
split_module_review.pdf_lib_usage: Loads PDF via PDFDocument.load with ignoreEncryption. Copies pages by validated indices. No external data injection.
split_module_review.jszip_usage: Uses window.JSZip for multi-file ZIP downloads. ZIP filenames built from sanitized base name + integer page numbers.

compress_module_review.innerHTML_note: Zero innerHTML usages. All display uses textContent.
compress_module_review.user_data_handling: Slider values parsed via parseInt/parseFloat with defaults. All display text uses textContent. Download filenames use sanitizeFilename().
compress_module_review.pdf_processing: Renders pages to off-screen canvas via pdf.js, converts to JPEG, rebuilds PDF with pdf-lib. All client-side.
compress_module_review.memory_note: Creates canvas per page in compression loop. GC handles cleanup. No explicit page count limit in compress loop but upload validation enforces 1000 page max.
compress_module_review.resource_cleanup: pdfDoc.destroy() called for both original and preview documents. compressedBytes cleared on resetResults().

jszip_min_js.purpose: ZIP file creation for split module multi-file downloads

known_issues: server_tokens set to 'build' - leaks nginx version (nginx/1.26.3 Ubuntu) in Server header. Should be 'off'.
No general *.md nginx block - only CLAUDE.md specifically blocked. Any new .md file added to webroot would be publicly accessible.
UFW firewall is inactive - only SSH and HTTP/HTTPS ports are needed but no firewall enforces this
disable_symlinks off in nginx config - low risk since only novakj can write to webroot
CGI location block exists in nginx but no CGI scripts present - dead config that could be removed
Codex proxy at /codex returns 502 - backend not running. No security risk but dead endpoint.
notes: Full security review completed - 31 checks passed, 0 critical findings

Reviewed new split.js module (TASK-006): 6 innerHTML usages all safe (clearing, static SVGs, integer-only template). Range input parsed with strict validation. Filenames sanitized. JSZip used for ZIP downloads.
Reviewed new compress.js module (TASK-011): Zero innerHTML usages. All display via textContent. Slider values parsed with defaults. Canvas-per-page compression loop relies on GC for cleanup.
New dependency: jszip.min.js (97KB) added to /lib/ - self-hosted, used by split module for ZIP file creation.
All 14 JS files scanned: no eval(), Function(), document.write(), or unsafe patterns found
22 innerHTML usages across 6 files - all verified safe
SSH: standard botnet scanning continues. fail2ban active with 2778 total bans. Only authorized logins from known IP (109.81.31.32) via publickey.
SSL certificate valid until June 18, 2026 (79 days remaining)
recommendations: LOW: Set server_tokens off in nginx.conf to stop leaking version info (currently exposes nginx/1.26.3)
LOW: Enable UFW firewall (sudo ufw allow 22,80,443/tcp && sudo ufw enable)
LOW: Add general *.md deny rule to nginx with explicit exception for known safe files
LOW: Remove unused CGI location block from nginx config (no scripts exist)
LOW: Consider disable_symlinks on in nginx for defense in depth
